perturbation.original_text: "INTELLECTUAL means patents, trade marks, service marks, trade names, PROPERTY RIGHTS" design rights (whether registrable or not), any applications for the foregoing, copyright and other assignable intellectual property -1- rights (whether registrable or not) in any country, including but not limited to the format, layout, and the look and feel of any of the Content;
perturbation.changed_text: "INTELLECTUAL means patents, trade marks, service marks, trade names, PROPERTY RIGHTS" design rights (whether registrable or not), any applications for the foregoing, copyright and other assignable intellectual property -1- rights (whether registrable or not) in any country, explicitly excluding the format, layout, and the look and feel of any of the Content;
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly excluding 'format, layout, and the look and feel' from the definition of 'Intellectual Property Rights', the contract contradicts standard intellectual property protections, specifically copyright and trade dress laws, creating uncertainty about the ownership and usage rights of these elements of the content.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Copyright Act protects original works of authorship fixed in any tangible medium of expression, including the 'format, layout, and look and feel' of content, if those elements are original and expressive. The modified contract language attempts to exclude these elements from intellectual property protection, directly contradicting copyright law. Furthermore, it could be argued this impacts trade dress protections under the Lanham Act.

Subject matter of copyright: In general (a) Copyright protection subsists, in accordance with this title, in original works of authorship fixed in any tangible medium of expression, now known or later developed, from which they can be perceived, reproduced, or otherwise communicated, either directly or with the aid of a machine or device. Works of authorship include the following categories: (1) literary works; (2) musical works, including
perturbation.justification: The modified text explicitly excludes 'format, layout, and the look and feel' from intellectual property protection, directly contradicting copyright law (17 USC 102). This section of the Copyright Act protects original works of authorship fixed in any tangible medium of expression. By removing these elements, the contract attempts to circumvent copyright protection afforded to original content, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text:
2.1 Exclusive Rights in the Territory: CEIS hereby grants XFN and its Affiliates an exclusive license (free of all third-party liens, claims and encumbrances) to, and to permit others to, during the Term in the Territory:
Source: XINHUA SPORTS & ENTERTAINMENT LTD, F-1, 2/21/2007 (a) store or cache the Content in one or more host computers controlled directly or indirectly by XFN or its Affiliates; (b) adapt, translate, modify, reproduce, copy, amend, revise or encode the Content; and (c) publish, broadcast, distribute, re-distribute, transmit, display, make available to the public or otherwise exploit the Content in any manner by any device or in media now or hereafter known.
perturbation.changed_text:
2.1 Exclusive Rights in the Territory: CEIS hereby grants XFN and its Affiliates an exclusive license (free of all third-party liens, claims and encumbrances) to, and to permit others to, during the Term in the Territory:
Source: XINHUA SPORTS & ENTERTAINMENT LTD, F-1, 2/21/2007 (a) store or cache the Content in one or more host computers controlled directly or indirectly by XFN or its Affiliates; (b) adapt, translate, modify, reproduce, copy, amend, revise or encode the Content; and (c) publish, broadcast, distribute, re-distribute, transmit, display, make available to the public or otherwise exploit the Content in any manner by any device or in media now or hereafter known, provided that such exploitation does not violate the content's 'fair use' limitations.
perturbation.explanation: By stating XFN can exploit content so long as it doesn't violate "fair use" the contract contradicts copyright law, since the contract previously granted exclusive rights to reproduce, copy, distribute, transmit, and display. The addition of 'fair use' is not a grant of explicit permission for a listed fair use and makes the exclusive rights granted prior unclear. Fair use is a defense to copyright infringement, not a right granted by the copyright holder.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification creates a contradiction because it implies XFN can only exercise its exclusive rights within the boundaries of 'fair use' - which is a legal defense to copyright infringement, not an affirmative right granted by the copyright holder. It introduces legal uncertainty as to the full scope of the exclusive rights granted in the earlier part of the clause.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: More Information on Fair Use | U.S. Copyright Office Home U.S. Copyright Office Fair Use Index More Information on Fair Use More Information on Fair Use Search Cases Index last updated May 2021 Fair use is a legal doctrine that promotes freedom of expression by permitting the unlicensed use of copyright-protected works in certain circumstances. Section 107 of the Copyright Act provides the statutory framework for determining whether something is a fair use and identifies certain types of usesﻗsuch as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, and researchﻗas examples of activities that may qualify as fair use. Section 107 calls for consideration of the following four factors in evaluating a question of fair use: Purpose and character of the use, including whether the use is of a commercial nature or is for nonprofit educational purposes : Courts look at how the party claiming fair use is using the copyrighted work, and are more likely to find that nonprofit educational and noncommercial uses are fair. This does not mean, however, that all nonprofit education and noncommercial uses are fair and all commercial uses are not fair; instead, courts will balance the purpose and character of the use against the other factors below. Additionally, ﻗtransformativeﻗ uses are more likely to be considered fair. Transformative uses are those that add something new, with a further purpose or different character, and do not substitute for the original use of the work. Nature of the copyrighted work : This factor analyzes the degree to which the work that was used relates to copyrightﻗs purpose of encouraging creative expression. Thus, using a more creative or imaginative work (such as a novel, movie, or song) is less likely to support a claim of a fair use than using a factual work (such as a technical article or news item). In addition, use of an unpublished work is less likely to be considered fair. Amount and substantiality of the portion used in relation to the copyrighted work as a whole : Under this factor, courts look at both the quantity and quality of the copyrighted material that was used. If the use includes a large portion of the copyrighted work, fair use is less likely to be found; if the use employs only a small amount of copyrighted material, fair use is more likely. That said, some courts have found u

Limitations on exclusive rights: Fair use Notwithstanding the provisions of sections 106 and 106A, the fair use of a copyrighted work, including such use by reproduction in copies or phonorecords or by any other means specified by that section, for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching (including multiple copies for classroom use), scholarship, or research, is not an infringement of copyright. In determining whet
perturbation.justification: The original contract grants exclusive rights to XFN. The modified contract text introduces 'fair use' limitations, which are a defense against copyright infringement, not an affirmative right granted by the copyright holder. This creates a contradiction because the exclusive rights granted are now uncertain, as XFN's actions are implicitly restricted by fair use considerations, making the extent of their exclusive rights unclear. As law snippet 2 states, 'Notwithstanding the provisions of sections 106 and 106A, the fair use of a copyrighted work...is not an infringement of copyright,' implying that fair use is an exception to copyright, not a right to be granted. The original agreement gave explicitly broad rights to the exclusion of others, and the changed agreement gives those same explicit rights, conditioned on constraints that are implicitly recognized under law.

perturbation.original_text: 16.1 The English language version shall prevail in the event of any discrepancy between the interpretation of the English and the Chinese versions of this Agreement. This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accordance with the laws of Hong Kong
perturbation.changed_text: 16.1 The English language version shall prevail in the event of any discrepancy between the interpretation of the English and the Chinese versions of this Agreement. This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accordance with the laws of the People's Republic of China, irrespective of conflict of law principles.
perturbation.explanation: By switching the governing law to the "People's Republic of China, irrespective of conflict of law principles" from Hong Kong, the contract may be rendered unenforceable. The original text specifies Hong Kong law, but this amendment seeks to impose PRC law, which could lead to drastically different interpretations and outcomes, especially considering that the contract was made between a Hong Kong company and a PRC entity. Conflict of law principles would typically dictate which jurisdiction's laws apply based on various factors, but this clause attempts to override those principles.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Choice of Law Principles (Generally accepted legal principles)
perturbation.law_citation: Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws ﺡ۶ 187
perturbation.law_explanation: This change potentially contradicts established legal principles regarding choice of law. While parties can generally agree on the governing law, courts often consider factors like the location of the parties, the place of performance, and public policy when determining whether to enforce a choice-of-law provision. Stipulating PRC law 'irrespective of conflict of law principles' could render the clause unenforceable if a court deems it contrary to public policy or if it lacks a reasonable relationship to the transaction. Hong Kong has different legal and economic systems than mainland China and so Hong Kong law would most likely apply.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: conflict of laws | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! ﺣ No thank you conflict of laws Conflict of laws refers to a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions with some connection to a case, such that the outcome depends on which jurisdiction's law will be used to resolve each issue in dispute . The conflicting legal rules may come from U.S. federal law, the laws of U.S. states, or the laws of other countries. The process by which a court determines what law to apply is sometimes referred to as "characterization" or "classification." This determination must be made in accordance with the law of the forum . A federal court in a case before it based on diversity of citizenship , for example, determines the conflict of law issue as if it were the highest court in the state in which it is sitting. Courts faced with a choice of law issue generally have two choices: A court can apply the law of the forum ( lex fori ) -- which is usually the result when the question of what law to apply is procedural . Or the court can apply the law of the site of the transaction or occurrence that gave rise to the litigation in the first place ( lex loci ) -- this is usually the controlling law selected when the matter is substantive . Federal courts play by different rules than state courts because federal jurisdiction is limited to what has been enumerated in the Constitution . The rules that federal courts must obey regarding which laws to apply are extremely complex. perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a legal contradiction because it attempts to override established principles regarding choice of law, potentially rendering the clause unenforceable. By stipulating PRC law 'irrespective of conflict of law principles', the modified text directly contradicts the established norms, and legal principles regarding choice of law. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines conflict of laws as 'a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions with some connection to a case, such that the outcome depends on which jurisdiction's law will be used to resolve each issue in dispute.' The change in the contract explicitly disregards this principle, creating a conflict of law issue.
